info.title: Ververica Unified Streaming Data Platform API
info.description: The REST interface for the Ververica Unified Streaming Data Platform that lets you provision, operate, and observe Apache Flink workloads at scale.

post.description:
This endpoint generates an access token for authenticating requests to the API.
Clients must provide credentials, a social login token, or a refresh token
depending on the selected authentication flow.

The access token returned in the response should be included in the
`Authorization` header as a Bearer token when calling protected endpoints.

credentials.description:
Login using classic username and password.
Typically used by users with registered accounts.

social.description:
Login using an OAuth2 social provider (e.g. Google).
The `externalAccessToken` must be obtained from the identity provider.
(Note: Currently, only `google` is supported.)

refresh.description:
Refresh an existing session using a refresh token.
(Note: This flow is not implemented yet.)

post.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Change password for an authenticated user using the current (old) password.
This flow is only available for users who authenticated using credentials authentication flow.

**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Initiates the password reset process for a user using the credentials authentication flow.
The user must provide the email address associated with their account.
If the email exists, a password reset link with an email token will be sent to the user via email.

204.description: The password reset request was accepted. A reset message has been sent.

post.summary: Set password within sign up and forget password flows
post.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Sets a new password for a user as part of the sign-up or forgot password flows.

This endpoint is typically called after the user clicks a link from an email,
which includes a temporary token. The token must not be expired or invalid.
Once the password is successfully set, the user's status becomes active.

This flow applies only to users in the credentials authentication flow.

204.description: The password was successfully set, and a confirmation email was sent.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Returns a list of workspaces accessible to the authenticated user.

This endpoint allows optional filtering by workspace status, permission,
or specific instance IDs. It also supports disabling runtime enrichment
to improve response times when such information is not required.
The response includes all workspaces that are either owned by the user
or explicitly shared with them.
Useful for checking whether workspaces are ready (status `OK`) or still being provisioned (status `PROCESSING`).

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description:
This parameter is obsolete.
The user ID is determined by the logged-in user performing the request.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.description:
A list of workspace IDs to return.
If no workspace IDs are provided, all workspaces shared with the user are returned.

parameters.name: statusCategory
parameters.description:
A list of workspace statuses to filter the list of workspaces to return.
If no workspace statuses are provided, all workspaces are returned.

parameters.name: withRuntimeInfo
parameters.description:
Whether to enrich the response with runtime information about the workspace.
This is enabled by default, but can be disabled to speed-up queries.

parameters.name: permission
parameters.description:
A permission code to filter the list of workspaces to return.
If provided, this can be used for example to return only the workspaces
where the user is an admin.

vvc-payment-system-user.description: PAYG and RC workspaces using Ververica AWS & Azure clusters

workspaces.name: Pay as you go Azure workspace
Pay as you go AWS workspace

post.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Creates a new workspace for the authenticated user based on the
provided configuration.

The workspace is created asynchronously — after
the request is accepted, the workspace will be in a PROCESSING state

You must wait until the workspace reaches an OK state before it can be accessed\
and used.

The `billingPlanId` is required to create the workspace and
determines the offering type and available resources.

To retrieve the list of available billing plans and their corresponding identifiers, use
the **Billing Plans API**.

For workspaces using the **Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC)** offering type,
the following additional fields are required:

- `clusterId`: ID of the pre-provisioned BYOC cluster.
- `data.storage`: Object defining the BYOC storage configuration.

To retrieve the list ofavailable BYOC clusters and their IDs, use the **Clusters API**."

pay-as-you-go.description: Creates a new workspace with the PAYG offering type.

reserved-capacity.description: Creates a new workspace with the RC offering type.

bring-your-own-cloud.description: Creates a new workspace with the BYOC offering type.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Retrieves the workspace identified by its ID.

The response contains the current configuration and status of the workspace, including offering type,
billing details, and deployment-specific metadata.

Useful for checking whether the workspace is ready (status `OK`)
or still being provisioned (status `PROCESSING`).

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

delete.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Deletes the specified workspace by its ID.
This operation is irreversible — all associated resources, configurations, and stored data will be permanently removed.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

A deployment target refers to a specific Kubernetes namespace or cluster where 
Flink jobs can be deployed.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all session clusters within the specified workspace and namespace.
Session clusters are long-running, reusable Flink clusters that can execute multiple jobs.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

post.description:
Deploy a new Flink session cluster within the specified workspace and namespace. 
The session cluster can be reused for multiple job submissions and is configurable.  
**Note:** The `spec.versionName` field is required to specify the engine version to use.  
To retrieve the list of available engine versions, use the `flink-version-support` API.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

get.summary: Get a session cluster by name
get.description: Retrieve the configuration and status of a session cluster by its unique name within the specified workspace and namespace.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique name of the session cluster.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a session cluster by name. This operation removes the cluster and releases its resources.  
**Note:** The session cluster must be in the `STOPPED` state before it can be deleted.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique name of the session cluster.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

patch.description:
Modify the configuration or metadata of an existing session cluster. 
This allows you to change its state, number of task managers and other configuration.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique name of the session cluster.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

post.description:
Creates a new deployment within the specified namespace and workspace.  
A deployment defines the job to be executed, its configuration, execution mode (STREAMING or BATCH),
associated artifact, resource settings, Flink configuration, and other runtime properties.  
Deployments are scoped within a **namespace**, which is a logical grouping of resources inside a workspace.  
In **Ververica Cloud**, the default namespace is `"default"`. Unless explicitly created or specified otherwise,
all deployments are placed in this default namespace.
**Note:** The `engineVersion` field is required to specify the Flink engine version to be used for the deployment.  
To retrieve the list of available engine versions, use the `flink-version-support` API.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

remote-jar-at-session-cluster.description: Creates a new deployment using remote jar URI.

artifact-jar-with-dependency.description: Creates a new jar deployment using uploaded artifact and dependency.

sql-script-deployment.description: Creates a new deployment using SQL script.

sqlArtifact.sqlScript:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE server_logs ( 
    client_ip STRING,
    client_identity STRING, 
    userid STRING, 
    log_time TIMESTAMP(3),
    request_line STRING, 
    status_code STRING, 
    size INT
) WITH (
  'connector' = 'faker',
  'fields.client_ip.expression' = '-',
  'fields.client_identity.expression' =  '-',
  'fields.userid.expression' =  '-',
  'fields.log_time.expression' =  '-''}',
  'fields.request_line.expression' = 'request_line_xpression''}',
  'fields.status_code.expression' = 'status_code_expression''}',
  'fields.size.expression' = '-''}'
);

CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE client_errors (
  log_time TIMESTAMP(3),
  request_line STRING,
  status_code STRING,
  size INT
)
WITH (
  'connector' = 'blackhole'
);

INSERT INTO client_errors
SELECT
    log_time,
    request_line,
    status_code,
    size
FROM server_logs
WHERE
    status_code SIMILAR TO '4[0-9][0-9]';

remote-jar-at-session-cluster.description: Created new deployment using remote jar URI.

artifact-jar-with-dependency.description: Created new jar deployment using uploaded artifact and dependency.

sql-script-deployment.description: Created new deployment using SQL script.

fields.log_time.expression =  -},

CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE client_errors (

INSERT INTO client_errors

delete.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Deletes the specified artifact by its `filename` from the given workspace and namespace.
This operation permanently removes the artifact from storage, making it unavailable for future deployments.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Returns full metadata for a specific artifact identified by its `filename` within the given workspace and namespace.
This endpoint provides detailed information about the artifact, including its `size`.
Use this endpoint when you need accurate artifact size not available in the list endpoint.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: filename

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Retrieves a list of artifacts for a given workspace and namespace.

Each artifact represents a file (e.g., JAR, Python script, or CDC config)
stored in the artifact store.

The list contains basic metadata such as `filename`, `uri` and `createTime`.

The `size` field is always set to `0` in this response for performance reasons.

To retrieve full artifact metadata including actual size, use the `getMetadata` endpoint.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

post.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Saves or updates metadata for an artifact.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Generates a temporary signature used to securely upload an artifact file
(such as a JAR, UDF, connector, or catalog) to or from the artifact store associated with
a given workspace and namespace.

This endpoint returns a set of presigned URLs along with required credentials and metadata
(e.g., access key, policy, and signature) that can be used for uploading a new artifact or 
retrieving an existing one.

These signatures are short-lived and grant time-limited, secure access to specific files
without exposing permanent credentials.

The file type must be explicitly specified (e.g., `ARTIFACT`, `UDF`, `CONNECTOR`), and the
filename must match the target file in the artifact store.

This is typically used as part of the deployment creation flow.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: fileName

parameters.name: fileType
parameters.description: fileType

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Logical namespace within the workspace used to organize and isolate resources.

aws-s3.description:
Example response when using AWS S3 as the artifact storage (`kind: s3i`).
Includes pre-signed URLs for secure uploading and downloading via HTTP PUT/GET.
This example does not require explicit access keys or security tokens
because the pre-signed URLs already include temporary credentials.

azure-abfss.description:
Example response when using Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (`kind: abfss`) as the artifact store.
Includes Shared Access Signature (SAS) URLs that provide time-limited upload and download access
without exposing permanent credentials.
Azure-specific URL encoding is used, and the `artifactPath` follows the `abfss://` URI scheme.

post.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Creates a new user account via the Ververica sign-up flow.
The endpoint supports two registration flows:
- `form`: Standard form-based sign-up requiring email and optional personal details.
- `social`: Social login using an OAuth provider (`google` or `github`) and an external access token.

social.description: Social login using an identity provider

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Returns detailed information about a user profile identified by the given user ID.
This endpoint can be used to fetch the full account information including user name,
email, company, country, billing data, and more.

vvc-payment-system-user.description: A user signed up via the standard sign-up flow with Ververica's internal payment system.

aws-marketplace-user.description: A user provisioned via AWS Marketplace with an active subscription.

delete.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Marks the specified user profile as deleted, identified by the given user ID.
Once marked as deleted, the user loses access to the system and their profile becomes inactive.
This is a logical deletion — the data is retained but the account is no longer usable.

patch.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Updates selected fields in the user profile. Only the fields included in the request
will be modified. This allows users to update their name or marketing consent status
without affecting other profile data.

get.description:
Retrieves metadata about all available Flink engine versions in the specified workspace.
Each engine version contains information about its type, status, supported features, and compatibility level.
This endpoint is typically used to:
- Fetch the list of available engine versions for use in session clusters or deployments.
- Identify the default engine version.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workspace that groups related namespaces and resources.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Retrieves a list of clusters registered by the authenticated user within the Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC) deployment model.

post.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Registers a new Ververica Cluster (Agent) in the BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) model.
This endpoint creates a logical cluster entry in the Ververica Control Plane before the actual agent is installed.
The cluster registration generates an instance ID that must be used during the Helm-based deployment.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Retrieves details of a specific Ververica Cluster (Agent) by its UUID.

delete.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Deletes a Ververica Cluster (Agent) identified by its UUID.
This removes the cluster metadata from the Ververica Control Plane.

patch.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Updates mutable fields of a Ververica Cluster (Agent), such as the name.

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Returns a list of billing plans available to the user, filtered
by optional parameters.

A **billing plan** defines how a workspace is billed and aggregates the following:
- **Region**: The cloud region where the workspace will be deployed
- **Offering type**: PAYG (Pay-as-you-go), RC (Reserved Capacity), or BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud)
- **Payment system type**: e.g., VVC, AWS Marketplace, Azure Marketplace
- **Billing items**: Detailed pricing for compute and traffic"

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: User ID for permissions check

parameters.name: offeringId
parameters.description: Filter billing plans by offering

parameters.name: regionId
parameters.description: Filter billing plans by region

parameters.name: shippableOnly
parameters.description: Returns only shippable billing plans if set to true

get.description:
**Not available for Ververica Platform: Self-Managed**

Returns a list of available cloud regions in which workspaces can be provisioned.

billingPlanId.description: Determines offering type, offering, and region

UserSetPasswordRequest.description:
Payload used to set a new password during account activation or password reset.
The token must be obtained from a link sent via email and must not be expired.

token.description: One-time token from the email link, used to authenticate the password change request.

password.description:
The new password to be set. It must be between 12 and 128 characters long,
and contain at least one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, one digit,
and one special character.

email.description: The email address associated with the user's account.

oldPassword.description: The user's current password. It is required to authenticate the password change.

newPassword.description: The new password to be set. Must be between 12 and 128 characters long, and include at least one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, one digit, and one special character.

WorkspaceUpdateRequest.description:
Number of Compute Units (CUs) to allocate for the workspace.
Each Compute Unit corresponds to a fixed amount of physical resources, based on the following formula:

**1 CU = 1 vCPU core + 4 GiB RAM**

The value must be greater than 0.  
For example:
  - `1.0` CU = 1 core, 4 GiB RAM  
  - `4.0` CU = 4 cores, 16 GiB RAM

UserProfileUpdateRequest.description: Request payload to partially update a user's profile information. Only the provided fields will be updated.

fullName.description: The user's full name, used for display purposes.

marketingConsentAcceptance.description: Indicates whether the user has accepted marketing communications.

currentAgentVersion.description: The version of the installed chart. Will be null on PENDING cluster

ComputeUnits.description:
Number of Compute Units (CUs) to allocate for the workspace.
Each Compute Unit corresponds to a fixed amount of physical resources, based on the following formula:

**1 CU = 1 vCPU core + 4 GiB RAM**
For example:
  - `1.0` CU = 1 core, 4 GiB RAM  
  - `4.0` CU = 4 cores, 16 GiB RAM

used.description: Number of Compute Units, indicating how much of the allocated resources are already consumed.

total.description:
Total number of Compute Units allocated to the workspace.
Represents the resource limit available for deployments.

Workspace.description:
A workspace is an isolated environment for deploying and managing applications,
typically scoped to a specific user, team, or project. Workspaces are associated
with a cloud region, a billing plan, and a compute cluster.

creatorId.description: UUID of the user who created the workspace.

name.description: Human-readable name of the workspace. Only letters, numbers, dashes (“-”), and underscores (“_”) are allowed.

userRoleId.description:
Role of the current user in the workspace.
Possible values:
  - `admin`
  - `owner`
  - `editor`
  - `viewer`

billingPlanId.description: Identifier of the billing plan assigned to this workspace.

offeringType.description:
Type of offering associated with the workspace.
- `PAYG`: Pay-as-you-go model.
- `RC`: Reserved capacity model.
- `BYOC`: Bring your own cloud infrastructure.

regionId.description: Identifier of the cloud region where the workspace is deployed.

creationTime.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the workspace was created.

deletionTime.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the workspace was deleted, or null if still active.

consoleUri.description: Direct link to the workspace view in the Ververica console.

clusterId.description: UUID of the cluster the workspace is assigned to.

WorkspaceStatus.description:
Workspace status.
When a workspace is being created, its status is in the PROCESSING category (name: "Creating").
Once the workspace is ready and usable, the status changes to the OK category (name: "Running").
Only when the category is OK can the user enter and interact with the workspace.

Artifact.description:
Represents a single file stored in the artifact store for a given workspace and namespace.
Artifacts can be JARs, Python scripts, SQL scripts, CDC definitions, or other supported formats.
They are used in deployments or data processing configurations and are uploaded separately via presigned URLs.
Each artifact contains metadata including upload time, uploader identity, storage location (URI), and logical classification (`kind`).

createTime.description: Timestamp when the artifact was uploaded.

filename.description: Name of the file (excluding the path).

size.description: Size of the file in bytes.

uri.description:
Full URI to access the artifact from within the system or deployment definition.
This is typically in the form of:
- `s3i://...` for AWS S3
- `abfss://...` for Azure
example: "s3i://vvc-tenant-u6qci2cabsd2a6s2-eu-west-1/artifacts/namespaces/default/FlinkQuickStart-1.0-SNAPSHOT.jar"

Artifact.title: Artifact

Signature.description:
Contains metadata and credentials used to upload or download an artifact (e.g. a JAR file) 
to and from a cloud-based object storage.
This object is returned when requesting a signature for an artifact upload. It includes storage-specific 
information such as pre-signed URLs or temporary security credentials, depending on the underlying backend.

Supported storage backends include:
- **AWS S3** (`kind: s3i`) – uses pre-signed URLs for PUT and GET operations
- **Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2** (`kind: abfss`) – uses pre-signed (SAS) URLs for PUT and GET operations

accessKeyId.description: Temporary access key used for uploads in OSS-based storage. Not used for S3 or Azure.

artifactPath.description:
Logical object path in the internal storage, used by the deployment system to locate artifacts.
This is not necessarily a directly accessible URL.

endpoint.description: Endpoint of the object storage service (e.g., for OSS). Typically null for managed S3 or Azure storage.

filePath.description: Internal file path used in certain OSS configurations. Unused for S3 and Azure.

kind.description:
Identifier of the object storage type, such as:
- `s3i` (AWS S3), 
- `abfss` (Azure Blob/File System).

policy.description: Upload policy definition (mainly used in OSS scenarios). Not applicable to S3 or Azure.

securityToken.description: Optional temporary security token. Not used for S3 or Azure.

signature.description:
Optional request signature for OSS-based uploads.
Not applicable to S3 or Azure.

Signature.title: Signature

flow.description:
Identifies the type of login request.
- `credentials`: Login with user email and password.
- `social`: Login with Google email account.
- `refresh`: Obtain a new token using the refresh token from a previous login request.

externalAccessToken.description: Access token of external identity provider, when flow=="social"

LoginResponse.description: Response containing access token and refresh token with metadata

accessToken.description: The access token issued to the user. This token is used as a bearer token to authenticate subsequent API requests.

refreshToken.description: The refresh token issued to the user. This token is used to obtain a new access token when the current one expires.

tokenType.description: The type of the token. Currently it's fixed and always set to "Bearer".

expiresIn.description: The duration (in seconds) for which the access token is valid.

refreshExpiresIn.description: The duration (in seconds) for which the refresh token is valid.

flow.description:
Registration flow type:
- `form`: Traditional form-based sign-up.
- `social`: Sign-up via an external identity provider (OAuth).

companyName.description: Name of the company the user represents.

requiredConsentAcceptance.description: Indicates whether the user has accepted required terms and policies.

marketingConsentAcceptance.description: Indicates whether the user agrees to receive marketing communication.

firstName.description: User's first name (relevant for form-based flow).

lastName.description: User's last name (relevant for form-based flow).

provider.description: OAuth provider used in the `social` registration flow. Required only when `flow` is set to `social`.

externalAccessToken.description:
OAuth access token used for social login.
Required when `flow` is `social`.

createdDate.description: Timestamp of user creation in ISO8601 format.

UserProfileStatus.description:
Represents the current status of the user's profile.
- Created: The profile has been created successfully but is not yet fully active.
- Active: The user profile is fully active and has full access to the platform.
- Frozen: The account is temporarily disabled (e.g. due to payment or policy issues).
Note: Status "New" is not currently supported in this context.

creationTime.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created (ISO8601 format).

freeTrialPeriodFreezingStartTime.description: Timestamp when free trial freezing started.

freeTrialPeriodFreezingEndTime.description: Timestamp when free trial freezing ended.

marketingConsentAcceptance.description: Indicates if the user accepted marketing communications.

paymentSystem.description: Payment system used by the user.

FreeTrialPeriodStatus.description:
Indicates the status of the user's free trial period:
- NOT_STARTED: The trial has not yet begun.
- ACTIVE: The user is currently in the trial period.
- FREEZING: The trial is temporarily paused.
- EXPIRED: The trial has ended.
- LIFTED: The trial period has been overridden or resumed.

PaymentDetailsStatus.description:
Describes the current state of the user's payment details:
- NONE: No billing details have been provided.
- VALID: The user has submitted valid billing details.

AuthenticationMethod.description:
The method used to authenticate the user:
- CREDENTIALS: Standard email/password login.
- GOOGLE: Login via Google account.
- GITHUB: Login via GitHub account.

MarketplaceSubscription.description:
Describes a subscription acquired via a cloud marketplace such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
Includes metadata such as account, product, user, and customer identifiers, status,
and timestamps of creation and deletion.

MarketplaceSubscriptionProvider.description:
Specifies the cloud provider through which the subscription was obtained:
- AWS: Amazon Web Services Marketplace
- AZURE: Microsoft Azure Marketplace
- GCP: Google Cloud Platform Marketplace

Autopilot.description: Autopilot configuration for resource recommendations and scaling.
Autopilot.title: Autopilot

parallelism.description: Degree of parallelism for job execution. Determines how many parallel tasks will be run concurrently.

BasicResourceSettingSpec.description: Specification of resources allocated for the component including CPU and memory settings.

cpu.description: Number of CPU cores allocated to the component.

memory.description: Amount of memory allocated to the component, specified with units.

BasicResourceSettingSpec.title: BasicResourceSettingSpec

mode.description:
Deployment mode:
- `SESSION`: selects an existing session cluster where the deployment will be deployed.
- `PER_JOB`: does not use a session cluster; the deployment runs as a standalone job.

name.description:
The name of the deployment target identifying the target cluster or job.
For `SESSION` mode, this is the cluster name of the session cluster.
For `PER_JOB` mode, this is equal to the workspace ID.

createdAt.description: Timestamp (Unix epoch) when the deployment was created.

creator.description: Unique identifier of the user who created the deployment.

creatorName.description: Display name of the deployment creator.

deploymentHasChanged.description: Flag indicating whether the deployment configuration has changed since the last deployment.

description.description: Optional textual description of the deployment.

engineVersion.description: Version of the Flink engine used by this deployment.

executionMode.description: Execution mode of the deployment; either streaming or batch.

flinkConf.description: Custom Flink configuration properties applied to the deployment.

labels.description: User-defined labels (key-value pairs) assigned to the deployment.

localVariables.description: List of local variables available to the deployment for parameterization.

modifiedAt.description: Timestamp (Unix epoch) when the deployment was last modified.

modifier.description: Unique identifier of the user who last modified the deployment.

modifierName.description: Display name of the user who last modified the deployment.

name.description: Must only contain symbols ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]$, and no more than 128 characters

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace or logical grouping where the deployment is created.

priority.description: Priority level of the deployment, used for scheduling and resource allocation. It's 5 by default

referencedDeploymentDraftId.description: Identifier of the deployment draft referenced by this deployment, if any.

taker.description: Identifier or tag representing the entity currently holding or controlling the deployment.

workspace.description: Workspace ID associated with the deployment.

DeploymentKerberosConfig.description: Kerberos authentication configuration for the deployment.
DeploymentKerberosConfig.title: DeploymentKerberosConfig

annotations.description: Metadata key-value pairs. Includes creator & modifier info.

deploymentTargetResourceType.description: Resource type RESOURCEQUEUE, DEFAULT, or UNKNOWN.

isDefault.description:
Indicates whether this is the default deployment target for the workspace.
In Ververica Cloud, each workspace automatically uses a default deployment target
whose ID is the same as the workspace ID.

namespace.description: Namespace in which the deployment target is registered.

resourceVersion.description: Version number of the deployment target resource. Increments on change.

workspace.description: Workspace ID to which this deployment target belongs.

engineCompatibility.description: Specifies the version of the engine that the deployment target is compatible with.

kind.description: Specifies the type of deployment target. Currently, only "kubernetes" is supported.

storageUri.description: Storage location used by the deployment target.

zookeeperEndpoint.description: Provides the Zookeeper endpoint for distributed systems.

JobSummary.description: Summary of job status counts related to deployment.
JobSummary.title: JobSummary

clusterName.description: Name of the target Kubernetes cluster.

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace for resources to be deployed.

Logging.description: Logging configuration, including log profile and logger levels (e.g., log4j settings).
Logging.title: Logging

ResourceTuning.description: Settings related to resource tuning and optimization for this deployment.
ResourceTuning.title: ResourceTuning

SessionCluster.description:
Defines a Flink session cluster, which is a long-running Flink cluster capable of running multiple jobs. 
It contains metadata, configuration, current state, and runtime status of the session cluster.
SessionCluster.title: SessionCluster

SessionClusterMetadata.description: Metadata about the session cluster, including identifiers, timestamps, and additional annotations or labels.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the session cluster was created.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the session cluster.

name.description:
Name of the session cluster (unique within the namespace). 
Only letters (lower case), numbers, dashes (-) are supported, and the beginning and end must be letters (lower case) or numbers.

namespace.description: Namespace in which the session cluster is created.

resourceVersion.description: Version number of the resource. It increments with each update.

workspace.description: Workspace identifier that owns this session cluster.

SessionClusterMetadata.title: SessionClusterMetadata

SessionClusterSpec.description:
Desired specification for the session cluster including resource requirements, Flink image configuration, 
logging, and Kubernetes-specific settings.

deploymentTargetName.description:
Identifier of the deployment target where the session cluster should run.
In Ververica Cloud, each workspace has a default deployment target automatically created,
and its ID is the same as the workspace ID.

engineType.description: Type of engine used. `vvr` for Ververica Cloud Engine.

flinkConfiguration.description: Key-value configuration for the Flink runtime (e.g., metrics).

flinkImageRegistry.description: Container registry where the Flink image is hosted.

flinkImageRepository.description: Container image repository used for the session cluster.

flinkImageTag.description: Image tag indicating specific Flink and VVR version.

flinkVersion.description: Version of Apache Flink used in the cluster.

imageUserDefined.description: Indicates whether the Flink image is custom-defined (`true`) or provided by the platform (`false`).

numberOfTaskManagers.description: Number of TaskManager pods to launch in the session cluster.

resources.description: CPU and memory resources for jobmanager and taskmanager components.

state.description: Operational state of the session cluster.

versionName.description: Fully-qualified engine version name combining engine and Flink version. Required for launching the session cluster.

SessionClusterSpec.title: SessionClusterSpec

state.description: Current lifecycle state of the cluster.

StreamingResourceSetting.description: Resource settings specific to streaming execution mode.
StreamingResourceSetting.title: StreamingResourceSetting

EngineVersionMetadata.description:
Metadata describing a specific engine version, including type, version info, 
feature support, hot update capabilities, and lifecycle status.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the engine version

EngineVersionMetadata.title: EngineVersionMetadata

EngineVersionMetadataIndex.description:
Contains metadata for all available engine versions within a given workspace,
including the default engine version.

engineVersionMetadata.description: List of available engine versions along with their detailed metadata.

EngineVersionMetadataIndex.title: EngineVersionMetadataIndex

FlinkVersionSupportedFeatures.description: Boolean flags indicating which features are supported by a specific engine version.

supportAnalyzeColumnOrigins.description: Indicate that this flink version supports analyze column origins

supportDagExpansion.description: Indicate that this flink version supports DAG expansion

supportDynamicLogLevel.description: Indicate that this flink version supports dynamically configuring log levels

supportFindUsedCatalogs.description: Indicate that this flink version supports filtering the catalogs used in sql statements

supportHotUpdateConfig.description: Indicate whether this flink version contains hot-update configuration file

supportIntermediateResultObservation.description: Indicate whether this flink version supports observing intermediate results.

supportJvmDebug.description: Indicate that this flink version supports JVM debug

supportLogArchive.description: Indicate that this flink version supports log archive

supportLogPaging.description: Indicate that this flink version supports log paging

supportNativeK8s.description: Indicate that this flink version supports native k8s mode

supportNativeSavepoint.description: Indicate that this flink version supports making savepoints in native format

supportNodeStateTtl.description: Indicate whether this flink version supports configuring node state ttl

supportSqlConsole.description: Indicate whether this flink version supports SqlConsole

supportSqlEngineServerMonitor.description: Indicate whether this flink version supports monitoring apis, such as logs, flame graph.

supportStateCompatibility.description: Indicate that this flink version supports state compatibility even if the SQL has been modified.

useForJarFineGrained.description: Indicate that this image should be used for Jar Deployments. Exactly one built-in default image must set this flag, and either zero or one user-specified entries may set it.

useForSqlDeployments.description: Indicate that this image should be used for SQL Deployments. Exactly one built-in default image must set this flag, and either zero or one user-specified entries may set it.

supportCdcYaml.description: Indicate whether this flink version supports CDC Yaml artifact.

supportConvertCxasToCdcYaml.description: Indicate whether this flink version support converting CDAS/CTAS script to CDC Yaml.

supportHiveDialect.description: Indicate whether this flink version support hive dialect.

supportMaterializedTable.description: Indicate whether this flink version support materialized table.

FlinkVersionSupportedFeatures.title: FlinkVersionSupportedFeatures

supportJobParallelism.description: Whether job parallelism can be modified during runtime without a full restart.

supportVertexParallelism.description: Whether individual operator (vertex) parallelism can be adjusted dynamically.

supportedConfigs.description: List of configuration keys that can be updated dynamically while the job is running.

ClusterDetailedResponse.description: Detailed information about a cluster managed via the Ververica Agent in the BYOC deployment model.

example.name: byoc-cluster
example.helmValueContent:
pyxis-api-gateway:
  tls:
    caCertPem: 
    certPem: 
    keyPem: 
    accessToken: eyJhbG6IkpX...eHRl03QG5NPQ
  endpoint: pyxis.aws.ververica.cloud
global:
  clusterId: jbmvyabjfyichwpl
  clusterName: jbmvyabjfyichwpl
  imagePullSecretData: eyJWIzUkFZR...GRXYz0ifX19Cg==

instanceId.description: Internal identifier of the cluster instance.

creationTime.description: Timestamp when the cluster was created (ISO 8601 format).

lastSeen.description: Timestamp when the cluster last communicated with the Ververica Control Plane (ISO 8601 format). Currently it's always null.

status.description:
Current status of the cluster:
- `PENDING`: The cluster has been deployed but has not yet completed registration with the Control Plane.
- `ONLINE`: The cluster is active and the agent is successfully communicating with the Control Plane.
- `OFFLINE`: The cluster is registered but the agent is not currently communicating with the Control Plane.

currentAgentVersion.description: The version of the Ververica Agent currently installed on the cluster. Will be null if the cluster is in `PENDING` status.

mostRecentAgentVersion.description: The latest available version of the Ververica Agent supported by the system.

helmValueContent.description:
Content of the Helm values file used during the agent's installation.
This configuration defines how the Ververica Cluster (Agent) is deployed in the user's cloud environment.
The agent establishes a one-way connection to the Ververica Control Plane, allowing Ververica to manage and monitor the cluster in the BYOC model.

CreateClusterRequest.description:
Request body for creating a new BYOC Ververica Cluster (Agent).
Creating a cluster through this endpoint does not deploy any resources.
It registers metadata and generates a unique `instanceId` required to complete the Helm-based deployment.

PartiallyUpdateClusterRequest.description:
Request body for partially updating a Ververica Cluster (Agent).
Currently supports renaming the cluster. The update does not affect the agent running in the user's cloud.

name.description:
New name to assign to the cluster.
This value is used only for identification purposes in the Ververica UI.

ClusterListResponse.description: List of clusters registered by the user in the BYOC deployment model.

clusters.name: byoc-cluster
clusters.helmValueContent:
pyxis-api-gateway:
  tls:
    caCertPem: 
    certPem: 
    keyPem: 
    accessToken: eyJhbG6IkpX...eHRl03QG5NPQ
  endpoint: pyxis.aws.ververica.cloud
global:
  clusterId: jbmvyabjfyichwpl
  clusterName: jbmvyabjfyichwpl
  imagePullSecretData: eyJWIzUkFZR...GRXYz0ifX19Cg==

LoggingV2.description: Logging configuration, including profiles and log level settings.
LoggingV2.title: LoggingV2

id.description: Internal region identifier used when creating or configuring a workspace

shippable.description: Indicates if the offering is available for immediate provisioning (i.e., "shippable").

metadata.description: Additional properties like 'reservedCapacityInCUs' for RC workspaces.

id.description: Unique identifier of the billing item.

name.description: Human-readable name of the billing item.

unit.description: Unit in which the item is measured - `GB` for traffic, `CU Hours` for compute.

BillingPlan.description:
A billing plan aggregates pricing and configuration for a specific offering and region.  
It defines the available billable resources and payment system configuration.

id.description: Unique identifier of the billing plan.

name.description: Human-readable name of the billing plan.

billingSystemType.description:
Internal type of the billing integration.  
Examples: `VVC`, `AWS`.

availableForUndefinedPaymentSystem.description: Indicates if this billing plan can be used when the user has no assigned payment system.

shippable.description: If `true`, this billing plan is available for provisioning (can be selected by users).

items.description: List of billable items (compute, traffic) and their pricing.

type.description:
Type of the payment system used to process billing.  
`aws-marketplace` — integrated with AWS Marketplace  
`vvc-payment-system` — internal billing system

productId.description: Product identifier (e.g., AWS Marketplace product code).

amount.description: The amount in the specified currency

currency.description: The alpha-3 code (complying with ISO 4217) of the currency that qualifies the amount

items.name: Pay as You Go Traffic, Azure (Germany West Central)
Pay as You Go, Azure (Germany West Central)
Reserved Capacity, Network Transfer, EU West

AzureCredentials.description: Azure Credentials to setup private connection role
